explanations.1: Option Base 0 means array indices will start at 0 instead of 1, which is unusual in VBA where arrays normally start at 1.
explanations.2: Option Compare Text makes string comparisons case-insensitive, so "ABC" and "abc" are treated as equal.
explanations.25: Byte is a data type that holds whole numbers from 0 to 255; it's used here because the row count won't be very large.
explanations.28: shParams appears to be a worksheet object defined elsewhere; this line gets how many rows are in the named range.
explanations.56: IRibbonControl is a special object passed by Excel's ribbon system when a user interacts with a custom ribbon button or dropdown.
explanations.71: The ByRef keyword means the returnedVal variable will be modified inside this function and those changes will be visible to the caller.
explanations.82: index + 1 is used because ribbon callbacks use zero-based indexing, but Excel ranges use one-based indexing, so we add 1 to get the correct row.
